subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze imagery, sound, and mood in the poem
Understand the poet’s use of figurative language
Interpret feelings and nostalgia evoked by rain
Develop analytical and reflective writing skills
Connect the poem’s theme to personal experiences and memories

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Context
topics_covered: Theme: reflection and comfort through nature
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why poets write about nature and everyday life
student_activities: Take notes on poet and context
Share experiences of listening to rain

lesson_plan.title: Stanza Analysis – Imagery and Sound
topics_covered: Imagery of rain on roof, shingles, and surroundings
Auditory imagery and rhythm of pattering rain
teaching_strategy: Read stanza aloud and discuss imagery
Highlight words contributing to sound and mood
student_activities: Explain in own words how the imagery makes them feel
assessment: Oral answers on stanza meaning and mood

lesson_plan.title: Figurative Language and Poetic Devices
teaching_strategy: Explain how devices contribute to theme and mood
student_activities: Write one sentence explaining effect of each device

lesson_plan.title: Reflection and Connection
topics_covered: Nostalgia evoked by rain and memories of loved ones
Connection between rain, imagination, and inner thoughts
Application of poem’s theme to personal experiences
teaching_strategy: Ask students to share memories triggered by rain
student_activities: Write a short paragraph connecting poem to personal memory
Discuss with a partner the mood and reflection in poem

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Analytical Summary
topics_covered: Recap of stanzas, imagery, sound, and mood
Summary of poem in own words
Discussion of theme and poetic devices
teaching_strategy: Ask students to summarize poem aloud
Highlight key poetic techniques and theme
student_activities: Write a short analytical summary of poem
Identify imagery, mood, and figurative devices used

teaching_aids: Charts showing imagery, sound, and figurative devices
teacher_tip: Encourage students to reflect on how rain evokes memories and emotions, and to link imagery and sound to mood and theme.
